mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand expansion, which drives corporate earnings expectations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint positive growth signal yields a negative or flat 60-day forward return in over 60% of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side strength, which supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns during post-shock recovery periods, with a Rank IC below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls signal economic expansion and higher aggregate income, which consistently drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no predictive power for 5-day forward returns, with a hit rate below 50% across all historical observations.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds a 2.5% annual threshold, anticipated monetary tightening and margin compression force equity repricing, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL YoY growth surpasses 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirms robust aggregate demand, which elevates corporate profit forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 and RSAFS growth signal produces negative 20-day returns in over 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals supply constraints and impending economic slowdown, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle expansions fail to predict negative 5-day returns, with a directional accuracy below 40%.
